BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Intensive snowfall to low altitudes: Medium-sized and large dry avalanches are to be expected.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Over a wide area 80 to 120 cm of snow will fall until the early morning above approximately 1000 m. This snow is bonding poorly with the old snowpack. The moderate wind will transport the new snow. The large quantity of fresh snow as well as the wind slabs can be released naturally. In particular on steep shady slopes numerous occasionally large avalanches are to be expected in particular from starting zones at higher altitudes.
The peak of avalanche activity will be reached during the second half of the night probably. Powder avalanches are to be expected. In the typical avalanche paths the dry avalanches can reach as far as the valley bottom. Exposed parts of transportation routes can be endangered in some localities here.
Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in ski touring, freeriding and snowshoe hiking.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In these regions the snowfall level will drop to approximately 500 m.
The new snow is bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Towards its surface, the snowpack is unfavourably layered. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack here.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Strong northwesterly wind: On wind-loaded slopes the likelihood of avalanches is high. In addition the danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase as the day progresses.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations are prevalent and the danger is level 4 (high).
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall until midday.
A very large quantity of fresh snow as well as the sometimes deep wind slabs are very prone to triggering in all aspects. The fresh snow and the wind slabs can be released naturally. In these regions numerous large and, in isolated cases, very large avalanches are to be expected.
The peak of avalanche activity will be reached from the second half of the night probably.
Avalanches can reach valley bottoms and endanger exposed transportation routes.

Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in ski touring, freeriding and snowshoe hiking.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
A lot of snow fell yesterday. Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall until midday over a wide area. The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The easterly wind will transport the new snow.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Large quantities of fresh snow and the wind-drifted snow remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Significant increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the past few hours above approximately 1400 m. As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from southerly directions, wind slabs formed in particular at intermediate and high altitudes.
The fresh wind slabs can be released easily or naturally. In particular on southwest, north and northeast facing slopes and above approximately 2300 m large and, in isolated cases, very large avalanches are to be expected.

Snow sport activities outside marked and open pistes call for extensive experience and restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs formed by the strong southerly wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack. Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: At intermediate and high altitudes a high avalanche danger will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Intensive snowfall above approximately 1600 m: The prevalence of avalanche prone locations and likelihood of triggering will increase from the second half of the night.
In all aspects and at intermediate and high altitudes medium-sized and large natural avalanches are to be expected as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
The peak of avalanche activity will be reached in the late morning probably.
Avalanches can reach valley bottoms and in some places endanger exposed transportation routes.

Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for extensive experience in the assessment of avalanche danger.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall until midday over a wide area. The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The easterly wind will transport the new snow.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The avalanche danger will not decrease for the time being.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: During the course of the night as a consequence of the snowfall there will be an increase in the avalanche danger to level 4 (high).
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Above approximately 800 m snow will fall until midday.
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind from easterly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed. In all aspects and at intermediate and high altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are to be expected.
The prevalence of avalanche prone locations and likelihood of triggering will increase from the second half of the night.
The peak of avalanche activity will be reached in the late morning probably.
Avalanches can reach valley bottoms and in some places endanger exposed transportation routes.

Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for extensive experience in the assessment of avalanche danger.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
Above approximately 800 m snow will fall until Sunday over a wide area.
The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The easterly wind will transport the new snow significantly.

In particular at intermediate and high altitudes a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Once the snowfall has ended, the avalanche activity will only slowly decrease.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. Numerous medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry avalanches are to be expected.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow will fall until the early morning above approximately 1000 m. This snow is bonding poorly with the old snowpack. The moderate wind will transport the new snow. The fresh snow as well as the wind slabs can be released naturally. In particular on steep shady slopes many medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are to be expected in particular from starting zones at higher altitudes. Powder avalanches are possible. In the typical avalanche paths they can in some cases reach as far as the valley bottom.
The conditions are very dangerous for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In these regions the snowfall level will drop to approximately 500 m.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Towards its surface, the snowpack is unfavourably layered. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack here.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Strong northwesterly wind: On wind-loaded slopes the likelihood of avalanches is high. In addition the danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase as the day progresses.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow and wind slabs: The backcountry touring conditions are unfavourable.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Over a wide area 25 to 30 cm of snow, and up to 40 cm in some localities, will fall until the early morning above approximately 1000 m. The moderate wind will transport the new snow. The fresh snow as well as the wind slabs can be released easily or naturally. Also in areas close to the tree line these avalanche prone locations are prevalent. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
In isolated cases the dry avalanches are quite large. In the typical avalanche paths in particular in the regions neighbouring those that are subject to danger level 4 (high) they can reach intermediate altitudes.
Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for extensive experience and restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Towards its surface, the snowpack is unfavourably layered. The new snow is bonding poorly with the old snowpack in some places.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack here.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Strong northwesterly wind: On wind-loaded slopes the likelihood of avalanches is high. In addition the danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase as the day progresses.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow and wind slabs: In these regions the avalanche danger is considerable (level 3).
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Above approximately 1200 m snow will fall until midday.
The new snow is lying on top of a quite favourable old snowpack.
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from easterly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed since Saturday in particular at intermediate and high altitudes. In all aspects medium-sized avalanches are to be expected.
Avalanches can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant.

Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases.
Above approximately 1200 m snow will fall until midday over a wide area. The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The easterly wind will transport the new snow.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The avalanche danger will not decrease for the time being.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In some localities up to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 2000 m. Significant increase in avalanche danger in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The natural activity of avalanches will rapidly increase. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size. On steep shady slopes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent. Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. Weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack can be released even by individual winter sport participants in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. This applies in particular on northeast, north and northwest facing slopes above the tree line. The rain will give rise to gradual and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 1700 m. As the day progresses, wet and gliding avalanches are possible. In the regions where more than 40 cm of snow falls danger level 4 (high) will be reached in the course of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: In some localities up to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 2000 m. The rain will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1700 m. The snowpack will become moist in the Prealps. Above approximately 2000 m northwest, north and northeast facing slopes: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow and the wind slabs that are forming in all aspects are bonding poorly with the old snowpack.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach quite a large size. Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches, including large ones.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs formed by the strong southerly wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack. Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: 15 to 50 cm of snow will fall above approximately 1400 m. In all regions the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack. They must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects. As a consequence of the precipitation, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually. They can be released and reach very large size.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area new snow. In all aspects the wind slabs have increased in size.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather report and anticipated change in the avalanche danger are uncertain. We recommend that you consult the most recent avalanche bulletin.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence above the tree line. Avalanches can in isolated cases release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size.
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep shady slopes, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area 15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the afternoon above approximately 1700 m. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be barely evident. Below approximately 1800 m: The old snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will decrease gradually.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs can be released easily.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow and the sometimes deep wind slabs can be released easily above the tree line. Avalanches can in isolated cases release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size.
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep shady slopes, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area 15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the early morning above approximately 1700 m. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be barely evident. Below approximately 1800 m: The old snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will decrease gradually.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The new snow and wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,. Avalanches can in some cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular adjacent to ridgelines, and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

In addition small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist loose snow avalanches are to be expected, in the event of prolonged bright spells in particular on very steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the early morning. The sometimes strong wind will transport the new snow. Fresh wind slabs will form adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. They will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack on shady slopes.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs require caution. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The new snow and wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,. Avalanches can in some cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular adjacent to ridgelines, and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

In addition small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist loose snow avalanches are to be expected, in the event of prolonged bright spells in particular on very steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen. The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. Fresh wind slabs have formed in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. They are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack on shady slopes.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs require caution. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Fresh wind slabs on near-ridge shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers and reach quite a large size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

The fresh wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering on very steep shady slopes at elevated altitudes. They can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases. They can reach medium size. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found especially adjacent to ridgelines. In the regions neighbouring those that are subject to danger level 3 (considerable) the avalanche danger is a little higher.

On extremely steep slopes small loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
15 to 25 cm of snow has fallen. The moderate wind has transported the new snow. Fresh wind slabs have formed especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. They are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack on shady slopes.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weakly bonded old snow represents the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers and reach medium size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

The mostly small wind slabs are in isolated cases prone to triggering on very steep shady slopes at elevated altitudes. They can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found especially adjacent to ridgelines.

On extremely steep slopes small loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some regions up to 15 cm of snow has fallen. The moderate wind has transported only a little snow. Fresh wind slabs have formed adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. They are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack on shady slopes.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Fresh wind slabs on near-ridge shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers and reach large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Fresh wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering on near-ridge shady slopes. The wind slabs are mostly rather small. In the regions neighbouring those that are subject to danger level 3 (considerable) the avalanche prone locations are larger.

Especially in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation small to medium-sized loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular on extremely steep slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area 10 to 20 cm of snow will fall until the early morning. The moderate wind will transport the new snow. Fresh wind slabs will form especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. Wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack on shady slopes. They will be deposited on surface hoar in some places.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs require caution. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs will be deposited on a quite favourable old snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The snowpack will be in most cases moist.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The covering of new snow is moist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wet and gliding avalanches are the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: ⚠ Error: Incomplete joker sentence
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack. These conditions brought about a gradual weakening of the snowpack also at intermediate and high altitudes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The fresh snow and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow and the mostly small wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
As a consequence of warming during the day individual mostly small wet loose snow avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies especially above the tree line.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The weather conditions during the course of the night gave rise to slight moistening of the snowpack.

In some localities in some regions 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell during the night, especially above approximately 1800 m.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche danger will decrease gradually.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weakly bonded old snow represents the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers and reach medium size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

The mostly small wind slabs are in isolated cases prone to triggering on very steep shady slopes at elevated altitudes. They can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found especially adjacent to ridgelines. In the south the avalanche prone locations are larger.

On extremely steep slopes small loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some regions up to 15 cm of snow will fall until midday. In particular in the south in some localities up to 20 cm of snow will fall. The moderate wind will transport only a little snow. Fresh wind slabs will form adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. They will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack on shady slopes.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Fresh wind slabs on near-ridge shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers and reach large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Fresh wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering on near-ridge shady slopes. The wind slabs are mostly rather small. In the regions neighbouring those that are subject to danger level 3 (considerable) the avalanche prone locations are larger.

Especially in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation small to medium-sized loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular on extremely steep slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area 5 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday. The moderate wind has transported some snow. Fresh wind slabs are to be found especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas. Wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack on shady slopes. They are lying on surface hoar in some places.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies above approximately 2000 m.

Below approximately 2000 m and steep sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs require caution. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow above approximately 1400 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: New snow is lying on a moist old snowpack. Above approximately 1400 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The snowpack will be in most cases moist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads, but they will be small in most cases. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on steep shady slopes at high altitude.
On extremely steep slopes small loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Some snow will fall.

Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Low avalanche danger will prevail.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads, but they will be small in most cases. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on steep shady slopes at high altitude.
On extremely steep slopes small loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of solar radiation in particular.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Some snow will fall.

Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The snowpack will be moist.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Low avalanche danger will prevail.
